qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Server-Side Request Forgery (SSRF) in downloadable product samples permits unauthenticated access to internal network resources
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The advisory describes SSTI (CWE-1336) via template expression injection in order address fields rendered in the admin view. This finding describes SSRF via PHP copy() in ProductController's download-sample endpoint. Different vulnerability class (SSRF vs SSTI), different root cause (missing URL validation vs missing template neutralization), different code locations (ProductController.php download sample vs order address template rendering), and different CWEs (CWE-918 vs CWE-1336).

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Server-Side Request Forgery (SSRF) in customer downloadable products endpoint allows authenticated access to internal resources
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The advisory describes SSTI (CWE-1336) via template expression injection in order address fields rendered in the admin view. This finding describes SSRF via PHP copy() in DownloadableProductController's download endpoint. Different vulnerability class (SSRF vs SSTI), different root cause (missing URL validation vs missing template neutralization), different code locations (DownloadableProductController.php download vs order address template rendering), and different CWEs (CWE-918 vs CWE-1336).

target_advisory_judgement.reason: The advisory (GHSA-5j4h-4f72-qpm6) describes Server-Side Template Injection (SSTI, CWE-1336) in Bagisto where a low-privilege customer can inject template expressions (e.g., {{7*7}}) into address fields during product ordering, which are then evaluated and rendered in the admin order view, potentially leading to RCE. Both qualifying findings describe Server-Side Request Forgery (SSRF) vulnerabilities in the downloadable product download endpoints via PHP's copy() function with inadequate URL validation. Neither finding identifies the advisory's vulnerable code (order address template rendering), root cause (missing template expression neutralization), or vulnerability class (SSTI/CWE-1336).
evidence: Advisory CVE-2026-21448 describes SSTI via template injection in address fields during checkout/ordering in Bagisto
Advisory CWE-1336: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements Used in a Template Engine
Advisory root cause: template expressions in address inputs are evaluated (not escaped) when rendered in admin view
Finding 1 root cause: PHP copy() in DownloadableProductController.php with zero URL validation (SSRF)
Finding 2 root cause: PHP copy() in ProductController.php with bypassable DNS-based URL validation (SSRF)
No overlap in vulnerable functions, endpoints, code files, or root-cause mechanisms
